title: 21. Pianola Tab Architecture Definition
original_text: Done. I added your tab rule directly to the `Player Piano / Pianola` section in [Roadmap.md]... - first tab opens by default - maximum 8 MIDI tracks per tab - tracks 9-16 go to a second tab - tracks beyond 16 go to a third tab - the user must be able to click the tab they need to view the relevant instruments/tracks
expected_subject: docs(roadmap): define tab architecture for Pianola view
expected_body_lines: - Specify default first tab and max 8 tracks per tab rule
- Define overflow logic: tracks 9-16 to tab 2, 17+ to tab 3
- Require clickable tabs for easy navigation of instruments
- Establish build contract before implementing Pianola window
- No code changes; documentation and architectural planning only
expected_command:
git commit -m "docs(roadmap): define tab architecture for Pianola view" \
  -m "- Specify default first tab and max 8 tracks per tab rule" \
  -m "- Define overflow logic: tracks 9-16 to tab 2, 17+ to tab 3" \
  -m "- Require clickable tabs for easy navigation of instruments" \
  -m "- Establish build contract before implementing Pianola window" \
  -m "- No code changes; documentation and architectural planning only"
